title: ‘She doesn’t give up that easily’: France’s Le Pen eyes the Elysee
summary: Marine Le Pen, convicted of embezzling EU funds, announces her plans to run in 2027 after winning an appeal.
imageAlt: ‘She doesn’t give up that easily’: France’s Le Pen eyes the Elysee
aiSummary: Marine Le Pen, convicted of embezzling EU funds, announces her plans to run in 2027 after winning an appeal.
keyPoints: ‘She doesn’t give up that easily’: France’s Le Pen eyes the Elysee
whyMatters: Noticia de actualidad en el sector de internacional.

multimedia.alt: ‘She doesn’t give up that easily’: France’s Le Pen eyes the Elysee
multimedia.caption: ‘She doesn’t give up that easily’: France’s Le Pen eyes the Elysee

fullArticle:
Hours after an appeals court lifted a ban on Marine Le Pen’s eligibility for the next presidential election while upholding her embezzlement conviction, France’s far-right leader announced plans to run in 2027 – possibly with an electronic ankle bracelet.

“I am a candidate in the presidential election. I will not change my mind,” Le Pen told the TF1 television channel.

Europe’s right-wing forces had been watching closely as some had expected her to hand the reins to her protege, Jordan Bardella, who at 30 is 27 years younger than Le Pen, popular, and more savvy on social media.

Le Pen’s far-right National Rally (RN) party opposes globalisation and immigration and seeks stricter border controls and fewer ecological policies. She is the daughter of the party’s founder and has run for pres...
